div1.entryFree:
Stormy, 1) tempestuous, full of wind and rain: like a s. day, now wind, now rain, Ven. 965. s. blustering weather, Lucr. 115. the s. gusts of winter's day, Sonn. 13, 11. a s. day, which makes the rivers drown their shores, R2 III, 2, 106. a s. night, Per. III, 2, 4.
2) violent, passionate: if you give o'er to s. passion, H4B I, 1, 165. his s. hate, H6B III, 1, 155.
